title: Volume events do not include Attributes
body:
### Issue Description

Volume events behave differently from containers. 

```sh
$ podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.5.0
API Version:   5.5.0
Go Version:    go1.23.8
Git Commit:    0dbcb51477ee7ab8d3b47d30facf71fc38bb0c98
Built:         Tue May 13 20:00:00 2025
Build Origin:  Fedora Project
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

### create volume with labels
```sh
 podman volume create test -l foo=bar
app-test
```

### observe event with null Attributes
```json
{"Name":"vol-test","Status":"create","time":1750453757,"timeNano":1750453757972870908,"Type":"volume","Attributes":null}
```

```sh
 $ podman volume inspect  vol-test 
[
     {
          "Name": "vol-test",
          "Driver": "local",
          "Mountpoint": "/home/sbatsche/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test/_data",
          "CreatedAt": "2025-06-20T17:09:17.938316998-04:00",
          "Labels": {
               "foo": "bar"
          },
          "Scope": "local",
          "Options": {},
          "MountCount": 0,
          "NeedsCopyUp": true,
          "NeedsChown": true,
          "LockNumber": 9
     }
]
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman events --format json
2. podman volume create test -l foo=bar
3. observe event


### Describe the results you received

 event has null value for Attributes field

### Describe the results you expected

Labels are displayed in Attributes the same way they are for containers.

```json
{"ID":"759911dc28846138f66243ff4d785124b13b90a2ab971fd61f8619583f7a1d8e","Image":"docker.io/library/busybox:latest","Name":"test-sleep","Status":"create","time":1750455114,"timeNano":1750455114185727701,"Type":"container","Attributes":{"app":"example","mylabel":"value1"}}
```

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

Additional environment details

comments.body: @hexfusion can you try with v5.5.1. It should be there on f41 repos.

> [@hexfusion](https://github.com/hexfusion) can you try with v5.5.1. It should be there on f41 repos.

reproduced 
```sh
$  podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.5.1
API Version:   5.5.1
Go Version:    go1.23.9
Git Commit:    850db76dd78a0641eddb9ee19ee6f60d2c59bcfa
Built:         Wed Jun  4 20:00:00 2025
Build Origin:  Fedora Project
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

```sh
$ podman events --format json
{"Name":"test3","Status":"create","time":1750685305,"timeNano":1750685305784347540,"Type":"volume","Attributes":null}
```

If you can provide a coderef and verification it is not expected. I can take a shot at resolution. thanks

@hexfusion can you confirm, bc I see it this way, that even containers don't return their labels in events?  I do see them in free form in the journal, for example, but not in `podman events --format json`
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Thanksfor the remnder going to retest

I think this issue is still present. As @hexfusion posted in the original description, the `Container` events definitely include the labels in their attributes. 

These are some events from some quadlet testing I've been doing recently.

```json
{
  "ID": "0c99a689d084c01e8fdbf269b2f5a8d8a3855ec7ad2216cfefd6352ab20f4efc",
  "Image": "<some_image>",
  "Name": "systemd-my-app-test",
  "Status": "start",
  "time": 1762967827,
  "timeNano": 1762967827004161000,
  "Type": "container",
  "Attributes": {
    "PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT": "my-app-test.service",
    "custom_label": "my-app-12345"
  }
}
```

vs
```json
{
  "Name": "systemd-my-app-test",
  "Status": "remove",
  "time": 1762967818,
  "timeNano": 1762967818264530000,
  "Type": "volume",
  "Attributes": null
}
```

I could be looking in the wrong place, but in libpod it appears that container labels are explicitly set on the events: 
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/events.go#L65

while I was there I noticed this: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/events.go#L126-L131 which could be problematic as the Events struct is defined via composition https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/events/config.go#L23-L65 and the following snippet loses the `execID` key
```go
	e.Attributes["execID"] = sessionID

	e.Details = events.Details{
		Attributes: c.Labels(),
	}
```
https://go.dev/play/p/YEnWFVCT0qR

anyway, the rest of the events types don't seem to specify any labels:  

[Network](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/events.go#L139)
[Pod](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/events.go#L164)
[Volume](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/events.go#L185)
[Secrets](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/events.go#L195)
